observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Inventory Committee Chairperson (i) prepare a detailed proposal to the Municipal Mayor to assemble a special team and schedule the conduct of a complete physical count of PPE which includes all types of PPE as reflected in the Financial Statements; and (ii) upon approval of the Municipal Mayor, proceed with the physical count and reconcile the results with the records of the Municipal Accountant;

Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant jointly prepare the Property Cards, PPELC and RPLC per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and

Municipal Mayor consider initiating the creation of a plantilla position for and hiring of a Municipal General Services Officer pursuant to Section 490, Article 20 of RA No. 7160 to ensure efficient administration of all properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
For CY 2020, the Management failed to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and failed to maintain the required property and equipment records as discussed under Audit Observation No. 4 in Part II of this AAR.

Other specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports thereon with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments;

Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit to the Audit Team a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects; and

Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to ensure prompt delivery of basic services and facilities to the public and achieve the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC formulate integrated economic, social, physical and other developmental plans and policies for consideration of the Municipal Development Council in coming up with a comprehensive local development plan that is compliant with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;

Municipal Development Council exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and public investment programs to ensure that the 20% DF is used solely for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and

Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all claims charged to the 20% DF are supported with a duly approved Program of Work and detailed accomplishment report establishing the nature of works performed as a capital expenditure.
observations.reason: Specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2019 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: The Management failed to submit the necessary documents stated in the audit recommendation.

The Management failed to submit the necessary documents stated in the audit recommendation.

Other specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Human Resource Management Officer observe prompt submission of ARA to GSIS whenever there are newly hired employees, salary adjustments, and/or other changes in members’ data in accordance with Section 4 of GSIS MC No. 3, series of 2012, to ensure timely updating of the GSIS database and to prevent inaccuracies in the billing statements;

Municipal Accountant prepare a detailed report on the unremitted GSIS contributions, duly supported with a reconciliation of past billing statements and remittances; and

Acting Municipal Treasurer, based on the report prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit all the premium contributions from prior periods that are due to GSIS.
observations.reason:
Unremitted premium contributions on previous years were still not remitted to the GSIS.

Other specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

Unremitted PhilHealth contributions on previous years were still not remitted to the PhilHealth.

Other specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer:

submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not remitted intact to the BIR on the prescribed period;

cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible penalties and surcharges for late payments; and

henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to remit all the withholding taxes as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned department heads to observe timely submission of reports and documents, especially when requested by the Audit Team, to allow timely review of municipal accounts and transactions including the preparation and transmittal of the annual audit report.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit on time the complete financial reports and supporting schedules including the financial transaction documents for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2018 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period on the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to submit the AAPSI, hence action taken on the audit recommendations contained in the CY 2018 AAR could not be validated.

Similar audit observation was noted in CY 2020 as discussed in Audit Observation No. 8 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the GLs and SLs on all cash accounts and the monthly BRS for all cash-in-bank accounts for CY 2020 and submit them to the Audit Team for review; and (ii) henceforth ensure that the GLs and SLs are regularly updated and the BRS are prepared every month; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a certified true copy of Cash in Bank cashbooks as of December 31, 2020; (ii) submit a reasonable explanation for not being able to deposit intact all collections; and (iii) deposit all cash under her custody to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
send demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2020 except those who have already rendered full settlement thereof in CY 2021;
cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who still failed to render full settlement of cash advances despite the issuance of demand letter pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
henceforth, (i) refrain from processing grant of additional cash advance to any accountable officers who have not yet fully settled their previous cash advances; (ii) consider implementing the “no liquidation no cash advance policy” by issuing the “Certification that previous CAs have been liquidated and accounted for in the books” pursuant to Item 1.1 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (iii) strictly impose the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in order to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) apply the perpetual inventory method of accounting for supplies and materials and strictly adhere to the recording guidelines and procedures on the receipts and consumption/distribution of inventories as provided under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and (ii) provide adequate disclosures for Inventories in the Notes to Financial Statements;
Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a GSO, prepare and maintain Stock Cards for each type of supplies and materials; and
Municipal Mayor, (i) require the Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral physical count of all supplies and materials and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnish the Audit Team; and (ii) consider creating a plantilla position for a GSO and the hiring of a qualified person for that position, subject to availability of funds.

We recommended that the:
Inventory Committee Chairperson (i) prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office, copy furnish the Audit Team and (ii) reconcile the results of physical count with the property and accounting records; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant fast track the preparation of the required PPELC, RPLC and PC and henceforth maintain and regularly update them in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
Members of the Sanguniang Bayan of Cagayancillo, Palawan submit a reasonable explanation on why they failed to pass an ordinance creating a special account in the general fund for the BGCM;
Municipal Budget Officer henceforth exert diligent effort to study the budgetary implications of proposed legislation and submit comments and recommendations thereon to the SB; and
Municipal Accountant observe strict adherence to the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording revenues including transfers and subsidies from the National Government.

We recommended that the:
LDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, (i) prepare the required investment plan for the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱9,266,895.65; the investment plan should correspond to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2020 totaling ₱662,917.00 and should be accompanied by a written justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of the fund; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all the proposed programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality; and
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in coordination with the LDC and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP therefor and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval should they find it reasonable based on the current circumstances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned department heads to observe timely submission of reports and documents, especially those requested by the Audit Team pursuant to existing accounting and auditing regulations, to allow timely review of municipal accounts and transactions including the preparation and transmittal of the annual audit report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2019 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the GLs and SLs on all cash accounts and the monthly BRS for all cash-in-bank accounts for CY 2019 and submit them to the Audit Team for review; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that the GLs and SLs are regularly updated and the BRS are prepared every month; and

Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) submit to the Audit Team the certified true copy of Cash in Bank cashbooks as of December 31, 2019 and a reasonable explanation for not being able to deposit intact all collections; (ii) deposit all collections under her custody to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury account; and (iii) henceforth, observe daily preparation and submission of RCD to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the GL, SLs and BRS for the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason:
Due to lack of manpower, the GLs, SLs, and BRS for the General Fund and Trust Fund were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

Likewise, the required copy of cashbooks and reports as to full accounting of Cash in Local Treasury as deposited to the bank were not yet submitted as of this writing.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the Furniture and Fixtures and Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account balances.
observations.reason: Delayed liquidation of cash advances was similarly observed in CY 2020 and thereby reiterated under Audit Observation No. 2 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Health Officer submitted to the Audit Team a certification that the drugs and medicines
requested for procurement in CY 2019 fall within and conform with the current edition of
PNF Essential Medicines List with the exception of eight drugs and medicines which
were not found in the said list but were recognized to be essential drug in the delivery of
health service in the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Other concerned municipal officials failed to submit the required documents and explanations stated in the audit recommendation.

Other specific actions taken by the Management could not be determined or validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the monitoring reports on the implementation of government PPAs for CY 2019 in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Municipal Engineer submitted the required monitoring reports for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MPDC, in coordination with the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB):

exert due efforts in finalizing the Municipality’s 10-year solid waste management plan and submit the same to the PCSD for approval; and

initiate the establishment of a municipal solid waste disposal facility, subject to pertinent guidelines issued by the PCSD.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to submit the 10-year solid waste management plan to PCSD for review and approval on January 19, 2021.
observations.reason: The Management is still in the process of revising the 10-year solid waste management plan based on the comments and recommendation of PCSD.

observations.managementAction: The GPB for CY 2020 was certified and officially endorsed by the DILG.
observations.reason: The Municipal GFPS Executive Committee and TWG did not submit the required explanation for their failure to consider the unappropriated amount of ₱98,780.28 during preparation of GPB for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to determine the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.  The Inventory Committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.      We also reiterated our recommendation that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required property cards and ledger cards.  Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of the actual count with the property and accounting records.  All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately.

Furthermore, we reiterated that the physical count of PPE should be undertaken annually by the Inventory Committee and the corresponding RPCPPE be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as required under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason:
The Management is currently creating a new Inventory Committee.

Reiterated in Finding No. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all the collections under their custody with the authorized government depository bank, in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.  Likewise, she should submit an explanation letter for not depositing the collections intact and promptly.

We also reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to remind the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their respective books of accounts.  Any discrepancy noted should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and take appropriate action thereon.
observations.reason:
The undeposited cash collections pertain to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

Reiterated in Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.  Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances, else suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash advances have been fully settled and liquidated.

We also recommended that the Management should see to it that officers and employees who will retire, resign or transfer to other agency are required to settle first their accountabilities before the issuance of clearance certificates to them.  Failure to do so will make the approving authority jointly and solidarily liable for the amount cleared as provided for under Section 104 of PD 1445.

Moreover, the Management should make other appropriate action such as, but not limited to, institutions of sanctions as provided for under Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason:
No justification/ reason were offered by the Management. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds were still not reconciled in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal Accountant to immediately complete financial reports and other documents required to be submitted under Section 70 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 21 of PPSAS No. 1.  Otherwise, suspension of the payment of salaries of the Municipal Accountant is recommended until she has complied with the submission of required reports and documents. Other actions which are deemed necessary should also be done in order to comply with the aforementioned law and rules.
observations.reason: Reports were still not submitted on the prescribed period due to lack of manpower in the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements (all funds) for CY 2017 including the duly certified copies of bank statements.  Henceforth, Bank Reconciliation Statements should be submitted within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: Reports were still not submitted on the prescribed period due to lack of manpower in the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should submit the AAPSI to the COA Audit Team timely, completely and accurately. The said AAPSI must indicate thereto the following: 
 
a. Reference Number; b. Audit Observations; c. Audit Recommendations; d. Agency Action Plan (Action Plan, Person Responsible and Target Implementation Date); e. Status of Implementation; f. Reason for Partial/ Delay/ Non-Implementation, if applicable; and g. Action Taken/ Action to be taken.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.   We also recommended the immediate submission of the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason:
Copies of contracts and purchase orders were still not submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2018.

No justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) should prepare plans, such as but not limited to: (1) Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for the integration in the local development plan, (2) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and (3) other relevant plans for the allowable programs for the children under Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. In accordance with the said plans, the indicated programs, projects, and activities should be properly disbursed and implemented with due regard to the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to submit the requested documents.

No justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the preparation and submission of the documents as prescribed under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the said Joint Circular.

We recommended the Municipality through its Local School Board to formulate strategic planning such as Comprehensive Development Plan, Local Development Investment Plan and other relevant planning.

We also recommended that the disbursements to be made out of SEF must be within the budget and in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal Treasurer, to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the Special Education Fund
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the “General Appropriations Act of FY 2018” for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees for CY 2018 which is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized deductions based on the above order of preference presented.
observations.reason: According to the Management, employees receive their take home pay less than ₱5,000.00 since their loans were not yet fully settled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant for the immediate remittance of GSIS premiums and loans to the GSIS.

We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to adjust, trace and resolve the discrepancy on the Due to GSIS account balance and ensure that all transactions are properly recorded.

Henceforth, we recommended for the Management to fully comply with the provisions of Republic Act No 8291 to avoid possible interest/penalty for late payments and/or possible misuse of the government funds.
observations.reason:
No justification was offered by the Management.

Reiterated in Finding No. 10.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management should prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.  In addition, we also recommend that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Likewise, the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱816,877.13 for CY 2017 should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank account.

We further recommended that the guidelines provided in NDRRMC-DILGDBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for the allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to submit the requested reports.

No justification was offered by the Management.

Reiterated in Finding No. 6.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to provide the requested report to the Audit Team.

No justification was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant prepare and immediately submit to the Audit Team the GL and SLs of Cash and Cash Equivalents accounts and Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts for CY 2018;
Municipal Treasurer (i) submit certified true copy of Cash in Bank cashbooks as of December 31, 2018; (ii) record only the collections and deposits in the  Cash in Treasury cashbooks in accordance with Sections 26 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II, and prepare the necessary adjustments for cash advances recorded therein; (iii) submit a reasonable explanation for not being able to deposit intact all collections; (iv) deposit all cash under her custody to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury account; (v) submit copies of RCD for the months of August to December 2018; and
Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate reconciliation of the monthly balances of cashbooks with the subsidiary accounting records.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and
Municipal Treasurer prepare Property Cards per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant:
prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances as of the end of each quarter for CY 2018;
impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to fully and promptly liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and
cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers who have unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018 and still failed to liquidate the same as of September 30, 2019.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor issue a demand letter to KUDZ Trading and Construction Supply ordering the refund of ₱749,918.59 for failure to execute the works in accordance with the Contract Agreement for the Design and Build Project for One Storey Evacuation Center for Municipality of Cagayancillo, Palawan; and
Municipal Engineer submit a satisfactory explanation for failure of the contractor to implement the project in accordance with the contract agreement and a narrative report on the actions taken by the Management to address the deficiency.

We recommended that the:
MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments; and
Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on the period prescribed under the same COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the MSWMB:
coordinate with PCSD personnel for the conduct of WACS and cause the preparation and immediate submission of the revised 10-year solid waste management plan of the Municipality to the PCSD;
assist the barangays in the re-establishment, operation and management of their materials recovery facilities; and
coordinate with PCSD, NSWMC and other government agencies for the establishment of a compliant solid waste disposal facility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee and the Municipal Accountant: (a) cause the immediate submission of the GPB, GAD AR and other GAD reports and documents requested by the Audit Team for CY 2018; and (b) henceforth, ensure timely submission of GAD related reports and documents in accordance with Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the unremitted balance of the Due to GSIS account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the remittance of GSIS contributions within the period prescribed under RA No. 8291 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR within the prescribed period to avoid possible interest/penalty for late payments and/or possible misuse of the government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of complete financial reports and supporting schedules including the financial transaction documents of all funds as required under Section 2.0 of COA Circular 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2017 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through its BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this Office within 14 days after the end of each semester

We recommended that the BAC of the Municipality should invite COA representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage. In the event that a procurement activity has to be postponed, the observers shall be notified immediately of the change in schedule.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should require their bidders to submit to the Audit Team the Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or to the public officers or employees mentioned above by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and the same should be submitted together with the required documentary requirements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) under Procurement Service of DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other than the PS depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or nonavailability of stocks in depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) at PS-DBM.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the Management invited observers in its procurement process and required the bidders to submit Sworn Affidavit that they are not related to the HOPE or to the public officers or employees by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree.
observations.reason: PMR for CY 2017 and 2018 were still not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring report as of December 31, 2017 was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Management to deduct and remit the PhilHealth contributions as prescribed by PhilHealth Circular No. 2017-0024 dated September 11, 2017, for CY 2018.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the said contribution on the prescribed period to avoid interest and surcharge, otherwise the Audit Team will issue a Notice of Charge (NC) for the interests and surcharges incurred for delayed remittance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that correct amount of contributions has been deducted from the Municipality’s officers and employees.
observations.reason:
The management still failed to remit the PhilHealth contributions on the prescribed period.

No justification/ comment was submitted by the management.

Reiterated in Finding No. 9.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee and require them to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to determine the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The inventory committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.

We also recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and maintain the required property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately.

Lastly, ensure that the physical inventory be undertaken annually and submit the RPCPPE to the COA Auditor concerned on or before January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.

We recommended the management to:

 coordinate with concerned agencies for the prompt approval and implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality;
 include in the municipal programs an information campaign on proper waste disposal to avoid hazardous effect of improper waste disposal to the community and environment; and
 initiate the immediate establishment of Solid Waste Disposal Facility.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year containing details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date.

Henceforth, we recommend strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to require the
Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to
determine the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The Inventory
Committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to
conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the
inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required
property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of
the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies
between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and
reconciled immediately.

Furthermore, we reiterated that the physical count of PPE should be
undertaken annually by the Inventory Committee and the corresponding RPCPPE
be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as required
under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer to deposit immediately all the collections under their custody with the
authorized government depository bank, in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, she should submit an explanation letter for
not depositing the collections intact and promptly.

We also reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to remind
the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile
their respective books of accounts. Any discrepancy noted should be investigated in
accordance with existing regulations and take appropriate action thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to strictly
enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances, else
suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash advances
have been fully settled and liquidated.

We also recommended that the Management should see to it that officers and
employees who will retire, resign or transfer to other agency are required to settle
first their accountabilities before the issuance of clearance certificates to them.
Failure to do so will make the approving authority jointly and solidarily liable for
the amount cleared as provided for under Section 104 of PD 1445.

Moreover, the Management should make other appropriate action such as,
but not limited to, institutions of sanctions as provided for under Section 5.1.3 and
5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal
Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds,
Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these
accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary
adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through its
BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the
GPPB and to this Office within 14 days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the BAC of the Municipality should invite COA
representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during eligibility
checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid
evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise, the BAC should see
to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a
sector and the other shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The
observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the
procurement stage. In the event that a procurement activity has to be postponed, the
observers shall be notified immediately of the change in schedule.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should require
their bidders to submit to the Audit Team the Sworn Affidavit that it is not related
to the HOPE or to the public officers or employees mentioned above by
consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and the same should be
submitted together with the required documentary requirements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should procure its
common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) under Procurement Service of
DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other than the
PS depot on the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-
availability of stocks in depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of
Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) at PS-DBM.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with applicable laws and
rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to compel the
Municipal Accountant to immediately complete financial reports and other
documents required to be submitted under Section 70 of NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I, Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and
Section 21 of PPSAS No. 1. Otherwise, suspension of the payment of salaries of the
Municipal Accountant is recommended until she has complied with the submission
of required reports and documents. Other actions which are deemed necessary
should also be done in order to comply with the aforementioned law and rules.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation
Statements (all funds) for CY 2017 including the duly certified copies of bank
statements. Henceforth, Bank Reconciliation Statements should be submitted
within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance with COA
Circular No. 96-011.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the
Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented
for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the the provisions of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. We also recommended the immediate
submission of the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their
supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the
Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipality through its Local Council for the
Protection of Children (LCPC) should prepare plans, such as but not limited to: (1)
Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for the
integration in the local development plan, (2) Local Development Plan for Children
(LDPC), and (3) other relevant plans for the allowable programs for the children
under Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012. In accordance with the said plans, the indicated programs,
projects, and activities should be properly disbursed and implemented with due
regard to the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the preparation
and submission of the documents as prescribed under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the said Joint Circular.

We recommended the Municipality through its Local School Board to
formulate strategic planning such as Comprehensive Development Plan, Local
Development Investment Plan and other relevant planning.

We also recommended that the disbursements to be made out of SEF must be
within the budget and in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and
Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal Treasurer,
to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the
Special Education Fund.

We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of
the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the “General Appropriations
Act of FY 2018” for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees for CY
2018 which is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized
deductions based on the above order of preference presented.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant for the immediate remittance of GSIS
premiums and loans to the GSIS.

We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to adjust, trace and
resolve the discrepancy on the Due to GSIS account balance and ensure that all
transactions are properly recorded.

Henceforth, we recommended for the Management to fully comply with the
provisions of Republic Act No 8291 to avoid possible interest/penalty for late
payments and/or possible misuse of the government funds.

We recommended for the Management to deduct and remit the PhilHealth
contributions as prescribed by PhilHealth Circular No. 2017-0024 dated September
11, 2017, for CY 2018.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to remit the said contribution on the prescribed period to avoid interest
and surcharge, otherwise the Audit Team will issue a Notice of Charge (NC) for the
interests and surcharges incurred for delayed remittance.

We recommended that the Management should prepare and submit the
LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In addition, we also
recommend that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Likewise, the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱816,877.13 for CY 2017
should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank
account.

We further recommended that the guidelines provided in NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for the
allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory
Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit the corresponding
reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445. Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances; else suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash advances have been liquidated.

We recommended for the Management to procure telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with the provisions of Appendix 13 of the IRR of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184). Likewise, Officials and Employees to be granted by the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with Item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be supported with Statements of Account/Bill from telecommunication service providers, official receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices printed in thermal papers should be photocopied.

Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services providers. The use/expenditure thereof not in accordance with RA 9184, pertinent COA Circulars and other applicable laws, rules and regulations may be subject to audit disallowance.

In addition, submit a copy of existing memorandum/order and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution Guidelines authorizing the claim of Telephone Expenses and its corresponding rate per entitled official/employee.

We recommended that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment under Procurement Service of DBM. On the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS). Those CSEs that the LGU already procured in violation of the provisions mentioned above, the management should send us the written explanation that would justify their action. Otherwise, the persons responsible would suffer the corresponding penalty prescribed by law.

Overall Recommendations:

6.1 Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP)
6.2 Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
6.3 Observers
6.4 Disclosure of Relations
6.5 Procurement Service (PS)

We recommended the Municipality to uphold and guide themselves with the applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR, respectively.

We recommended the Municipality to submit accurately, timely and completely the documentary requirements as prescribed by law, or rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Development Fund. The Local Development Council should prioritize and incorporate in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU those projects that are aligned with the stated regulation. Moreover, submit on the prescribed period the Report of Utilization for review and monitoring purposes, by the Audit Team.

Likewise, the management should maintain a special account for the 20% Development Fund to properly monitor the implementation of various development projects and in compliance with Chapter 6 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs Volume 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Acting Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to ensure implementation of programs, projects, and activities for the protection of children.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Local School Board should ensure the preparation of the Local School Board Budget every year for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development.

We further recommended that the Municipality should automatically release the proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF to the Local School Board, as mandated by Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.

We reiterated that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should (a) deposit all collections promptly and intact with the authorized government depository bank, in compliance with Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to avoid possible loss and misuse of funds; (b) properly maintain his cashbook by fund and regularly update, balance and reconcile his record with the cash on hand daily or as necessary; and (c) reconcile his cashbook with the records of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.

The management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer under his own accountability. Instead, the Municipal Mayor should designate a disbursing officer who shall perform the disbursing functions pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant should segregate the balance of withdrawals from actual cash collections of the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and reclassify the unsettled accountabilities to the appropriate account. Cash advances made by disbursing officers should be accurately recorded to the “Advances to Special Disbursing Officer” account.

We recommended that Management should ensure that the grant of cash advance to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer or the disbursing officer is supported with the necessary documentation and used for a legally authorized specific purpose, in compliance with Section 30-4.5 and 89 of P.D. No. 1445. All concerned officials are also enjoined not to approve transactions and disburse government funds without satisfying first the basic documentary requirements provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. The Municipal Accountant should only process those transactions that are in conformity with laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to update the RPT Account Registers, come up with accurate listings of the names of taxpayers, update changes in ownership of each individual taxpayer and compute the amount due and collectible for the year. He should then certify the list and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in setting up the accurate RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Sections 19(b) and 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant should effect adjustments to reflect the correct balances of Due to LGUs, RPT and SET Receivables and the corresponding deferred income accounts. She should likewise reconcile said balances with her subsidiary records and taxpayers index cards maintained by the ICO-Municipal Treasurer at the end of the year. Lastly, she should properly observe the percentage distribution of the proceeds of RPT and SET, as provided in Sections 36 and 93 of the said Manual and Sections 271(a) and 272 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the deduction of advance payment and retention money from every progress and final payment to contractors on account of infrastructure projects to ensure that uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities are covered, in compliance with Section 5.3 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to prove the regularity and validity of payments.

We also recommended strict compliance with the aforementioned regulations to ensure that only duly supported disbursement vouchers are processed and paid.

We recommended that management should strictly follow the provisions of Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the posting of the required warranty security to avoid incurrence of possible loss to the government.

We recommended for the concerned persons to render an explanation / justification on the above-noted discrepancy.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the completed infrastructure project in the General Fund books by debiting Water Supply Systems (1-07-03-040) and crediting Government Equity (3-01-01-010) by ₱9,383,670.00.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should observe proper recording of trust fund transactions, in compliance with Section 104 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the budget for the ensuing years are prepared in accordance with the procedures, schedules and local budget preparation forms prescribed under the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs and for the Sangguniang Bayan to require, prior to the approval of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets, that special purpose appropriations are categorized by object of expenditure and presented in a similar sequence as a department or office expenditure program except the staffing and compensation profile where there are no personnel and office to operate.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Budget Officer should strictly adhere with the mandates of R.A. No. 7160 and discontinue the practice of allowing non-capital outlay items to be included in the continuing appropriations. The unexpended balances of appropriations for non-capital outlay which are included in the continuing appropriations as of December 31, 2015 should be reverted to the General Fund and shall thereafter be available for expenditure through a subsequent enactment.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year containing details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date.

Henceforth, we recommend strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated that PPAs under the 20% Development Fund should strictly be in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. The Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the Budget Officer, should strictly observe that the appropriations for the 20% Development Fund be supported with identified, detailed and specific programs and projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP). The Local Chief Executive and the Budget Officer should likewise ensure that the use of such fund shall be restricted to those priority development projects identified under the said regulations.

We also recommend that management should maintain a special account for the 20% Development Fund to effectively monitor disbursements and to maximize the implementation of various development PPAs, thereby delivering optimum benefits to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strictly adhere with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the preparation of Annual GPB and GAD AR and submission thereof to the concerned agencies for review and approval before its incorporation in the Annual Budget.

The GAD Focal Person should determine/identify gender issues in accordance with the definition of the aforementioned guidelines to ensure that GAD fund allocation shall be utilized for PPAs that directly address gender disparities and promote gender equality, gender-responsive governance, women’s economic empowerment and protect and fulfill women’s human rights. The GFPS should also plan and provide a consistent and clear basis of setting up performance indicators, targets and budget to accomplish the target outcomes/outputs efficiently and effectively.

Lastly, proper training and seminar should be intensified to the members of the GFPS relative to GAD concepts, principles and policies, especially on GAD planning and budgeting to enhance and broaden their knowledge and proper execution of GAD PPAs.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor create an inventory committee and require them to conduct an
immediate physical count of all its PPE to determine the actual existence and
proper valuation of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, Volume 1. The inventory committee may coordinate and request the
assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all properties under
their respective offices and submit the inventory report to the committee for
validation and verification.

We also recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and maintain the required
property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of
the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies
between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and
reconciled immediately.

Lastly, ensure that the physical inventory be undertaken annually and
submit the RPCPPE to the COA Auditor concerned on or before January 31 of
each year.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following:
 Instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
regularly reconcile their respective books to accounts. Any discrepancy
noted should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and
take appropriate action thereon;
 The Former ICO-Municipal Treasurer to immediately turn-over all the
cash collections in his custody to the new ICO-Municipal Treasurer for
deposit to the authorized government depository bank intact, in
compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
Likewise, the Former ICO-Municipal Treasurer should submit an
explanation letter for not depositing his collections intact and promptly;
 The ICO-Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 59 of NGAS
Manual on the procedures for the stale checks. Accordingly, the
Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjustments in the
books to account for the stale checks, provided they are supported with
the original disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules
and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances,
else suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash
advances have been liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile
the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other
reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial
statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be
effected in the books of accounts.

We recommended for the Management to procure
telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance
with the provisions of Appendix 13 of the IRR of the Government Procurement
Reform Act (RA 9184). Likewise, Officials and Employees to be granted by the
Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with Item No. 5, Annex
D of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be
supported with Statements of Account/Bill from telecommunication service
providers, official receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as
required in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices
printed in thermal papers should be photocopied.

Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a
rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond
just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into
account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various
telecommunication services providers. The use/expenditure thereof not in
accordance with RA 9184, pertinent COA Circulars and other applicable laws,
rules and regulations may be subject to audit disallowance.

In addition, submit a copy of existing memorandum/order and
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution Guidelines authorizing the claim of Telephone
Expenses and its corresponding rate per entitled official/employee.

We recommended the management to prepare PPMP, consolidate it with
the APP that must be approved by the Municipal Mayor and submit it to COA
office timely, accurately and completely in the prescribed latest format issued by
the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB).

We recommended the Municipal Mayor through its BAC to maintain the
accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this
Office within 14 days of each semester.

We recommended that the Municipality BAC should invite COA
representative as Observer. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one
observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other
shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The observers shall be
invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage.

Thus, we recommended the Municipality to require their contractors/
suppliers to submit to us Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or to
the persons mentioned above by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil
degree and the same should be submitted together with the required
documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Municipality should procure its common-use
supplies, materials and equipment under Procurement Service of DBM. On the
grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of
stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure a Certificate of Non-
Availability of Stocks (CNAS). Those CSEs that the LGU already procured in
violation of the provisions mentioned above, the management should send us the
written explanation that would justify their action. Otherwise, the persons
responsible would suffer the corresponding penalty prescribed by law.

Overall Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipality to uphold and guide themselves with
the applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR, respectively.
Section 4(6) of PD 1445 states that “Claims against government funds shall
be supported with complete documentation.” In line with this, we
recommend the Municipality to submit accurately, timely and completely the
documentary requirements as prescribed by law, or rules and regulations. Section
39(3) of said decree provides “It shall be the duty of the officials or employees
concerned xxx to comply promptly with these requirements. Failure or refusal to
so do without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for administrative
disciplinary action xxx.”

Lastly, no public officials or employees should be pecuniary interested,
directly or indirectly, in any contract or transaction of the government in
violation of the laws such as Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public
Officials and Employees (R.A. 6713) and Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act
(R.A. 3019). Even if the public officials and employees of the Municipality don’t
know the law, they are still liable because Art. 3 of the Civil Code provides
“Ignorance of the law excuses no one from compliance therewith.”

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Management should
strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Development Fund. The Local
Development Council should prioritize and incorporate in the Annual
Investment Plan of the LGU those projects that are aligned with the stated
regulation. Moreover, submit on the prescribed period the Report of Utilization
for review and monitoring purposes, by the Audit Team.

Likewise, the management should maintain a special account for the 20%
Development Fund to properly monitor the implementation of various
development projects and in compliance with Chapter 6of the Manual on New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs Volume 1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, the
Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Budget Officer should strictly adhere
with the mandates of R.A. No. 7160 and discontinue the practice of allowing
non-capital outlay items to be included in the continuing appropriations. The
unexpended balances of appropriations for non-capital outlay which are
included in the continuing appropriations as of December 31, 2016 should be
reverted to the General Fund and shall thereafter be available for expenditure
through a subsequent enactment.

We recommended the management to:
 coodinate with concerned agencies for the prompt approval and
implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the
municipality;
 include in the municipal programs an information campaign on
proper waste disposal to avoid hazardous effect of improper waste
disposal to the community and environment; and
 initiate the immediate establishment of Solid Waste Disposal Facility.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Acting Municipal
Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going government
projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the
year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the aforecited regulations.
We also recommended the immediate submission of the CY 2016 copies of
government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the
Office of the Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at
least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to
ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of
children.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Local School
Board should ensure the preparation of the Local School Board Budget every
year for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and
repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research,
purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development.

We further recommended that the Municipality should automatically
release the proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property
accruing to the SEF to the Local School Board, as mandated by Section 272 of
R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should thoroughly review the travel expense claims, as part of the internal audit system of the Municipality to determine completeness of supporting documents, compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations on travel and correctness of computations, before its approval.

Management should also ensure that the provisions of the aforementioned regulations on travel are strictly complied with.

Henceforth, the concerned officials and employees should exercise prudence in approving travel orders and itineraries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the submission of financial statements, records and reports to the Commission on Audit (COA) from the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant in order that all financial reports and transaction documents are submitted on time.

The Municipal Accountant must ensure the completeness of all the documents supporting the disbursements, as well as the signatures therein, before it is processed for payment. The ICO-Municipal Treasurer should secure additional copies of DVs for his use in updating records so as not to delay the preparation and submission of financial reports.

We recommended that Management should immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE totaling ₱847,504.26 for CY 2015 to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liabilities- DRRM” in the trust fund books pursuant to Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to ensure that funds are available for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC in times of calamities and to avoid possible misuse. They should also maintain separate bank account for the special trust fund to ensure that the unexpended balance is properly recorded and accounted.

The MDRRMO should observe the monthly submission of RSUDRRMF to the Audit Team, as required under Sections 5.1.5 of the said COA circular.

We recommended that the Municipality should ensure that the disbursements to be charged under the current year’s LDRRMF and unexpended balances of previous years are aligned with the approved annual LDRRMP/LDRRMFIP.

The Municipality should also ensure that a comprehensive and integrated plan be prepared annually in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework and policies on the disaster risk reduction and management in close coordination with the LDRRMC. The plan should cover projects and activities to be funded by current year’s LDRRMF, as well as the unexpended balances of previous years upon recommendation of the LDRRMC.

Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of the aforementioned regulations on the allocation, utilization and management of the LDRRMF is hereby enjoined.

We recommended that the local school board should ensure the preparation of the local school board budget every year for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development.

We further recommend that the Municipality should automatically release the proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF to the local school board, as mandated by Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.
